star_1.text: Doesn't behave well on a Chromebook. It sometimes loses track that it was installed from Play Store and has to be uninstalled and reinstalled. It also randomly crashes with no error messages. Developer support was less than useless for these issues.

classic bait app, these things are a dime a dozen. they design the game for money spending making the difficulty level so high you want to spend money on it. it's a psychological reward box through monetary loss on your part. Then when you call them on it, they blame the player. Save your hard earned money, and throw this game in the trash where it belongs.
I really liked the game, except for the very stupid mechanic where players must repeatedly tap your towers to stop them from immobilization. Completely ruined it for me.
When you watch ads to get gems it just hangs and you have to close the game down. When you log back in however it doesn't credit you. Better to get the game on Steam instead.

Constant uninstalls/reinstalls needed on Chromebook. Every time I reboot my Chromebook, I have to uninstall and reinstall this app, because when I try to run it after a reboot it tells me that it wasn't installed from the Google Play Store, even though it was. So it takes uninstalling it and reinstalling it, and then I can play it again.

congratulations you've made the game worse, nice work. I remember playing it years ago, now the game has lost balance. you've made it more difficult so players spend more money. I understand your strategy ( the game is old let's change the banner, make the waves shorter, receive less gold from monsters so we can squeeze some more profit ). Clowns you've have destroyed one of the best classics. R.I.P. ORIGINAL KINGDOM RUSH :(

Loved it on armor games. Now its on my phone! Still lovin it! 8/27/17 Some years later I've decided to come back, but not with nostalgia but dread. All the other kingdom rush games are still pay to play, despite having some of the worst microtransactions in all of mobile. If the 2nd and 3rd games were made free, I'd completely understand, but all these years later you still have to pay to play the game on top of pay ridiculous amounts of money for the heros and other items. Make the games free.

Would be a decent game. I know as I have defeated the campaign, yet everytime I get online something is always different and NOT in a good way. I refer to the fact I had almost 4,000 diamonds saved up from completing the campaign and various run throughs. Log in today, all 4,000 have disappeared, my skills have been reset....?! Everything else is as it was as far as campaign/quest completion. This makes the 2nd time this has happened. How does this happen and why?? Diamonds have a shelflife ?!
Regardless of the towers you build, there are some attackers or characters you cannot kill, thus losing lives

horrible game balance. level 5 already screws you over, the items are 1 time use when the money pouch for example could be a 1 time buy to get that 500 more gold. placing the towers are horribly placed by the devs and cant be changed. does not let you play like you would like to play a tower defence game. if you want to lose your mind grinding then this is the game for you. if you are looking for a game to pass time then do not download this.

Been playing this game for years, spent quite a bit of money on coins as well. There's a new bug I'm running into. If you try to get 100 free coin, after watching advertisement, it gets suck on "processing your request". On occasion I need to get some free coin to take me to victory. I've watched at least 50 videos now where I don't get any coin. This only happens on adverts for other games. Normal video advertisements of products / businesses pay out the small 100 coin bonus. PLEASE FIX

They changed the game to make it pay to win. I played this game for days years ago and I tried it again. Campain is super easy now but you can no longer do endless without purchase from shop. Many would argue that you can get gems just by playing, but it's similar to what happened to many IPs. You either need mindless grind or shell some money. Very disappointed.

Can't buy gems the ability to buy gems goes away after the first game or 2.. makes no sense as they make money if they leave it. I want my score. I got 5 million, almost 6 on the endless... it's complete bs it says no in app purchases with the pp they just take the ability to get gems away and think people will still want to play.

Tactics & coding in game: cheap, cheating & in general no consideration to the ergonomics their 'gameplay mechanics' impact the GUI & 'potential' customers. Arthritis of my hands does not permit me to engage in tap-fests without severe consequences. I have big hands & digits so everytime i attempt i end up selling or randomly upgrading a tower -- kills enjoyment when postured to fail. To balance & fix: Shorten the time to 'counter lockdowns' & reduce the 'counter' tap-fest to 1-tap.

This game is a good example of a tower defense. The graphics are well done, and the sound is great. I recommend it. I'm giving it a 1 star rating because it crashed on me 4 times, and each time, the crash blew away a huge chunk of my crystal stash. It's an irritating experience to build up a bunch of crystals so you can trick out towers, and have a crash kill the game AND wipe out your stockpile. I lost most of the crystals I earned, by playing, to game crashes. A real bummer.

The easiest difficulty is impossibly hard. No strategy works whatsoever. I played for an hour and made zero progress; I couldn't even come almost close to beating a single level. Staggering, unbelievably punishing on the easiest difficulty. I'm genuinely mind blown. It's irritating because there isn't a way to earn the gems other than by playing but.. you can't play because you can't win. Awful game, I love a few of their games but this one is just awful. One of the hardest games I've ever seen.

Loved this game and was my go to for things like plane rides. Unfortunately, they've now made it so that the game has to check in with their server. I'm sure it's to peddle the DLC; there's no other reason as it's a (used to be) completely offline game. Because of this I've uninstalled the game and will no longer recommend it to others. Sadly, the hunt for a good offline time killer continues.

Maybe I was pampered by the tower defense games I've played on a real computer but the scaling in this game is terrible. Way too much clicking around, deleting and upgrading units, etc. Game must be constantly tended to and if you start sliding downhill at any level just quit and retry - there's no coming back. Not worth your time.

Rigged for failure It's not a bad TD, don't get me wrong, but I do not appreciate when a game instead of giving fair challenge, it forces you to fail and come back later to redonthe level once you have made more purchases and upgrades. It's cheap and stinks of cheating.

Game play is fine, ads mess everything up. I'm literally in the middle of a game and I use an ad to get gems to buy something to help me and the ad screws up the game I'm in.... Literally pissed because I was on the last wave, I'm just livid. I completely uninstalled this game... Don't even bother playing it!

star_2.text: DO NOT SPEND MONEY IN GAME!!!!! My friend was a part of making this game and he told me that as soon as you use bought items in game, it automatically increases the difficulty (making you still lose and keep wanting to spend more money) its a decently fun and basic TD game, but you are better off not spending any money and just earning by playing. would definitely give 5 stars if it weren't for the "casino" tactics.

SLOW GAME. Played game for maybe 2 weeks. I don't like how the rounds get longer and longer. Right now its 17 rounds of monsters. Short little attempts like 10 rounds max of monsters is fine. But, 17+ ! It feels like 20 minutes to the finish a level. This isn't a short game to play if you have a short 5 minutes of freedom. Takes too much time and not enough fun. For those reasons I'm outt

I really really really want to like this game. It has a great concept, it has good art, and it makes you actually think about strategy, but after playing seven or eight levels it becomes apparent that the only way to win is to watch ads for free gems to spend on boosts or to just pay for the gems outright to buy boosts. I tried several times to replay levels from earlier in the game where I had missed a star but even adding 500 gold to my starting purse didn't help.

GREAT Game, don't get me wrong, but there's a pretty annoying issue that happens when you get a call, or accidentally leave the app, that crashes the game, and doesn't allow you to resume where you left off, forcing you to start over... This is extremely inconvenient when you're super far into a game.

I've played this game for over 3 years. It used to be fun. It used to be a challenge. It also USED TO BE FREE! Now it's no longer fun BECUZ it is almost impossible to win unless you buy bombs or freeze wands. Good choice of crutches to help. You can earn them by saving diamonds the game gives free for playing. Or pay real money. And finally, MONEY. There are no ads in the entire game (thank you for that, I.H.) but I have to buy the same games I've had for 3 yrs.? My phone was stolen. No games.

For a game that has easy medium and hard, it's ridiculous that you can't even pass all the easy levels. Completing the 3 stars is easy, but the extra levels are ridiculously hard. This game needs to be adjusted to allow people to 100% complete the easy stage.

This could be a great game. It is obviously masterfully made, but, it's also deliberately been designed with horrible balance, so that children, out of frustration, are forced to purchase in game items to continue, and use up their parent's money. This is a real shame, and it's somewhat shameful and embarrassing for the developers. The game is solid, just deliberately made too hard, so you spend money. Would be a 5/5 if it were designed for actual, rewarding game play, rather than as a tool

The game is gun but the game rips you off when you watch the ad video for diamonds. I've watched the ads half a dozen times and each time the game just says processing your request. This message doesn't go away unless I back out. I've even let the game sit for well over 10 minutes on this screen. I guarantee the devs still get the ad revenue while I'm left without the diamonds that I was supposed to recieve.
Needs more updates!!! game was great but once you've maxed it out, its dead.

Most of the game is pretty fun, but the same strategy works for most every level, and the tap mechanic they randomly put in on some boss levels to keep towers from being inactivated is very annoying, doesn't add any fun, and also isn't very well implemented and often doesn't work. For that alone i docked two stars
Good at first, then like so many of this genre it becomes unbeatable without spending money. Even then, it's just too difficult and becomes very slow to progress to the point that it's just boring.

Did not care for this game. Its frustratingly hard. Every level you are just slightly too weak to do well. So you are constantly scrambling to survive. If barely squeaking out a win every level sounds fun to you, then by all means play the game. I dont want to have to keep replaying levels later on to get my 3rd stars. I want a strategy game that gives you a chance to get the 3rd star on the first try.

Brilliant game overall, but the amount of additional content that must be purchased is somewhat of a joke. The game is free to play but several heroes must be purchased additionally. I would happily pay £2 - £3 for the game and have all content available.

Nearly unplayable on newer phones. If the application loses focus, it freezes. This is incredibly frustrating, especially when it's so easy to make the switch applications gestures, which is enough to kill it and lose all progress. Look for a dev update on this one. The gameplay seems good. Microtransactions haven't been an issue an hour or two in on the hardest setting, but I expect to hit the microtransaction roadblock at some point. I'm hoping for paid version without microtrans.

Fun game, really fun game... Too bad it glitches out and deletes game info. It's happened twice, to bring all your apps in the Cascade mode while playing the game and accidentally swipe and close the game through that method, everything is erased. I've been so far twice and boom, it's all gone. Hard to want to play more.

For a game that claims its fair and balanced, that's complete BS. The easiest difficulty is still extremely hard. The enemies have insane amounts of health, the swarms come in huge waves at early levels. There's so much I could point out that's wrong but unfortunately Google won't let me put it all on the review. I'm giving its 2 stars because the concept is good, but the game itself is terrible. It's fun for a few missions and then you start to see all of the problems this game really has.

The game would be perfect if gems could be used for upgrading towers, instead of for purchasing special items. But the developers know what they're doing. The current system forces you to watch a ton of ads to progress, so they selected this specifically to make money. I totally understand, but it hurts enjoyment. Also, I guess their paid games follow the same logic? I'd happily pay if they don't.

Most of the base levels are fine but almost all of the bonus levels and challenges are rng fests, you can do the same thing 10 times and get a different outcome each time, tolerable when you have room for error but the "challenges" give you none so low rolling damage or bad targeting will force a loss. most are also just spamming stat monsters at you with only like 1 or 2 working build orders so there's no real strategy, just trial and error. good port of a flash game but not really much else

It's kinda cool, but it feels unbalanced. On both hard and normal modes the first level was cake, the second was harder by 100% and the third I could barely get 2 stars on. I'm no slouch when it comes to TD, but with this one there's just no time to build enough coin to put up or upgrade a tower before you get swarmed. I went through several times with different strategies and nothing seemed to help, by round 3 I felt like I was getting beat with no real solutions.

The Steam version of this game was amazing, and I was excited to play it on mobile but thos version to so scummy and is a scam. The game puts paywalls and ads in front of important unlocks even at the very beginning of the game. Edit: I understand the game is completely playable and beatable without purchases but the number of features locked behind paying money or watching adds turns me away from the game.

star_3.text: well put together tower defense game that plays as an ideal game of its sort, easily losing track of time in. As you progress, however, the pay to win wall creeps up on you. Rather sinister in its subtlety, I would suggest the game if not for that. If the game's intent was to entertain rather than to hook then bleed you dry. I'd have actually invested in it, would really like to see a game that's there to entertain rather than fleece. That sorta thing ppl would naturally support!

I actually enjoyed this game when I first downloaded it. Even gave them some money for a hero. But as I played on a couple things quit making sense. The points you start each round off are always different. Especially after you complete the initial 12 locations. There is not really enough points at the start to build up your forces to cover the multiple spawns and different enemies. I wish they would give you a higher number of points to start with especially at higher levels and difficulties.

3 stars. Watching ads to get the rewards is a 50/50 proposition at best. The ad plays with no problem, but when it comes time to get the reward, you get the spinning wheel of death half the time. No matter how long you wait, nothing happens. The star rewards for getting upgrades is kind of dumb also. Halfway through the game, everything is maxed out. So, trying to get the rest of the stars is kind of pointless.

I like this game, I remember playing it a lot back in the day. That said it doesn't seem to remember progress from the level I was in if I close out of the app for a while. Several times I've gone to do something else and came back to find my progress gone, one time when I was at 13 or 14 of 15 rounds done...

the initial campaign is fun and makes this a good game to play. However, as others have said - once you get past that you will not have enough coins to beat the more difficult levels. Essentially it becomes pay to win or in this case, pay to keep winning.
Overall great game, but the 'tap to unfreeze' mechanic is so infuriating it ruins levels for me. It turns a fun and classic strategy tower defense game into a frustrating carpal tunnel simulator.

Very addictive tower defense game. Love the animation and the voice-over, and the chance to play with different levels of difficulty. EDIT: down to 3. I have completed all the iron challenges, but it is impossible to pass the new campaigns,let alone with 3 stars. Plenty of stars, but there are no more upgrades to buy. Pity as I played for hours and would have continued if it wasn't so damn frustrating. And yes, I tried different strategies, but only one very specific strategy would work.

Good game! However, to pass the game or get into further levels, you need more gems. You can watch many ads to get your gems, but some of the ads freeze the app and needs to be restarted. Also, you don't get the gems when the app freezes. So you'll be watching ads with no payout. You can also purchase gems but it seems too pricey. If they were cheaper, people might actually buy more.
it was fun, but it became impossible very quick unless you spent alot of money on upgrades. Even as you go through the levels and upgrade it is still impossible to beat without spending money.
The game is good except I can't use the items that I bought with gems. I keep pressing on them in the game but nothing happens.

As another user mentioned, I'm on OnePlus 7 pro android 10. Game does not stay running in the background. If I come out of the game for any reason, I can't go back into it. Only way is to restart the game. Developers need to optimize this game. It's really annoying. Please fix. Otherwise game one of the best as it is simple yet challenging.

Fun game, really good and you could even play for hours. The reason I'm giving this a 3 star is because I wish there was a way we could get NEW heroes without using real money. Personally, I'm a free to play player. If this was added I would DEFINITELY give it a 5 star. I've been playing for years and maxed out I just with we could use more heroes. Please fix this.

Update: hard to believe it's been 12 years since my first review. Unfortunately this game has changed, updates to make it harder. I remember being able to beat most the levels without need for spending gems on stuff. Now it's impossible to win the later challenges without spending gems on bombs and stuff. Pay to win.

It was great until I defeated the grand wizard on the last part of the trail map. I have managed to beat a few of the newer challenges it gives you, but mother of God it is infinitely more stressful. I can play on one challenge for hours and still not beat it. I wouldn't mind so much, but the ONLY way to get more powerful is to beat the levels. You can buy power-ups, but by the time I get around to using them it is too late. Probably gonna end up uninstalling this game soon.

an interesting game let down by annoying mechanics. it's less of a tower defence than a puzzle game: there's a very limited figuration of towerd that will work for a given setting and a lot of the game is just restarting until you figure it out. there's little room or interest in e.g. play style or variation, so the game feels repetitive quickly after a couple hours.

The first 8 or so levels are fun. But, from that point on, the game becomes impossible and you cannot win the next levels unless you buy upgrades. There is literally no way to survive past a certain point without paying (and I suspect youd have to keep paying to win). You can't even grind through and earn enough in game credits to win. I eventually Uninstalled because I didn't see any point is trying any more.
could be a good game if I could see what I was doing. the graphics are so small that you can not see what is happening. if you want a mindless game, this game is for you, not me.

It's a solid game, but there is a big problem. If you pause and come back after 10+ minutes the game resets. The later rounds take some time, so you have to have time to play it through. It's not a game to casually play when you have 5/10 minutes free. (edit) I tried what the reply says and it did not change a thing.

very amazing awaomley nice game with many features and real time conflicts. has a variety of things to do and upgrade! The reason for my 3 star review is that back in the day when I got this game it was not free. I spent money to play it, but now it's free and has ingame things to purchase and it's truly disappointing. It is also the reason I did not purchase the followup:frontier

Great game, I love it alot. Good concepts, play styles, and strategies. A perfect tower dense game for anyone! My only complaint though is that once you beat the campaign, you get to do elite levels, which reccomend 50 stars or more for each level. The campaign only gives you about 30 stars. So its almost impossible to progress without paying money, or building up a huge dimond stash for an eternity jist to beat one level! Make some bridge levels to help progress players forward.

star_4.text: I remember this game from when I played it years ago, back in an age when Flash was supported. Upon finding out it had been moved to an app, I knew I had to download it and go through the nostalgia. The overall main game is enough to keep it interesting to the end, with maps having minor variances in mechanics along the way. The facts that the store is not required for the main game and that ads are 100% optional, they make this game, without a doubt, something to enjoy.

this game is really good. Just one gripe, whenever I play, the pause button is placed in such a way that even glancing it with a finger will trigger the menu. Combined with the fact that the two biggest buttons in the pause menu are "restart" and "quit," I get sent back to the battle menu much too often in the middle of a game. Please fix this, or at least add a "Do you really want to quit/restart?" before I get kicked out of a game I was doing really well on. Edit: Happened AGAIN!

Pretty solid TD game. My opinion it's better than the other 2 Kingdom rushes I've tried. I have not played all 5. You are able to watch ADS in this to accumulate gyms so that you can use the bonuses but sadly there's no way to get the other heroes which I was disappointed by. I think they should make the heroes purchasable with gems so that you can watch ads long enough and play whoever you want.

I first found this game on the armor games website many years ago and immediately fell in love; and back then they didn't have the heroes or a shop to buy items. I enjoyed the goofy violence, animations, dialogue, and even little Easter eggs in all of the different levels. I like it more now that I can play it on my tablet on my down time at home or on the go. Just wish they gave us more gems to work with like they use to. Guess they gotta pay the bills so they can keep the story rolling.

Fun, but pretty quick to finish. You don't have to buy anything. I finished all levels on casual and a handful on normal/hard within 2 weeks. It is challenging, but casual is doable with practice. The one downside, which I think they should change, is that there is no benefit to go for normal or hard difficulty. I 3 starred everything on casual, which allowed me to upgrade everything, so there was no point in doing the extra modes or doing them on a higher difficulty.

A great TD, not as good (not as many units or upgrades) as Bloons, but different enough to make it unique and very replayable. I own several titles from this game studio. Only thing I dislike is I paid for the game and there's (better) hero characters that can only be unlocked with paying more. Getting the "full game" is like buying a fleshed out demo. All their games have the same setup. With bloons you buy the game and all the units/characters can be unlocked without more purchases.

The first campaign is a blast! I love it! But then once I finished the sequence, it rolled to credits. More campaigns popped up. And maybe I just suck at games, but these campaigns get REAL hard. And I can't beat em unless I purchase a bunch of ammo with crystals. And those cost money. So it's the end of the road for me with this game. But I'm sure many of you will not find the advanced campaigns hard like I did.

Love this game and all of it's versions! Only reason it's not a 5 start review is because anytime you download it to a new device you've got to start over. There's no Cloud Storage. Also, if the characters you could buy were cheaper, or if you could purchase them with in game coin (even grinding out a bunch of money) would be great!

I like the game so far it has a cool art style and is pretty fun. The ads are optional, which I love, and if you do watch an ad, it gives you 100 gems, which helps a decent amount. There is a good amount of strategy which surprised me. The reason why I didn't give this a 5 star is because it's pretty dang hard. I watch a lot of ads to get gems to help me, which does take away a little from the experience, though. This is genuinely a good game, and I would recommend you check it out.

Loved the gameplay. The main game doesn't need crystals to beat, which is really great. When you start playing the side quest, then crystals come into play. However, you get crystals whether you win or lose a stage, or you can watch a 30 second ad. Or of course, pay for it. A few things I didn't like about the game was the constant tapping on my stations that were disabled by the enemy. Also it was, at times, difficult to deploy units to the bottom of the screen to stop enemies.

This is one of the now classic great Tower Defense games on mobile. And it's free too these days since they've released two newer versions of it with KR: Frontiers and KR: Origins. Not to mention the Scifi version Iron Marines. Which all of those are great too. Although I do find the micro-transactions in them on mobile annoying, they're not intrusive or convoluted like most mobile games. As a note all of them are also available on Steam for PC for purchase with everything unlocked. Great game!❤

Excellent game with good balance and fun heroes. Glad I happened across the first installment and I'm looking forward to the next ones. Previously, I gave the game a lower score after losing progress to a crash, but searching revealed that Android's Battery Optimization can cause such behavior when an app moves to the background. Edit: Still crashing. Game needs to auto-save on pause.

Overall I enjoy this game! I've played through the campaign twice and it didn't feel at all repetitive, I give it 4 stars only because I would rather get it for pc than mobile. I consider it a good game because unlike other td games your given abilities which are almost impossible to win the game without. I also enjoy that the power ups (Meteors,Reinforcements) are unpaid for and are given their own upgrade trees.

Fun, challenging but... Ok, i love tower defense games and this is one of the better ones. You really have to use strategy to win. There is no 'brute' force to win here. But after the main campaign, the extra quests are insane. You get swamped with swarms of enemies and unless every tower is maxed out, you stand no chance. And before you have enough to fully upgrade, they are pouring in relentlessly. I've tried many strats but frustration leads me to want erase the game thus only 4 stars.

Great game! Super addicting and the sound fx make me laugh. Four stars because while the game offers good challenge, sometimes you just do not have enough assistance to progress. You get a few coins per monster defeat and can take a few waves before they add up. The layout is also very tricky. You do not have enough coins to fill up the map for good fun. There are SEVERAL enemies with weaknesses that are introduced but not enough coins to progress efficiently. Endless waves can be a drag, sadly.

Classic TD. Hours of play, missions are not impossible and there is def no need to pay. I beat almost every mission without items and had plenty of gems saved to rerun the few I didn't. Game got 4 stars for 2 simple reasons. 1. I'm not buying heros make them obtainable through grinding. The packs you offer cost more than buying another one of your games which is ludicrous. 2. The no save, and random game crashes are a recipe for frustrated gamers. Call it 3 and 1/2 stars with me rounding up.

Looking for a great Tower Defense game to lose myself in. Playing this on the PC Google Games Beta with no issues thus far. The theme of the game, the story has my attention. I want to be the strategic hero for them. Playing casual mode because I am in it for the fun. This is an early review of the game as I have just begun to explore the first few levels. I believe i will lose myself to the many levels to come.

The game is fun and simple to play. You get 4 types of towers, which each have 2 specialties for level 4. Multiple abilities per specialty, so it makes the game more than a "build only one type of building" defense. I have 2 main complaints - the first is mele contact. It seems like the soldiers have to be in the center of the road to stop all enemies.Seen enemies walk right by. The second is the polymorph spell- soldiers/hero do not attack sheep, so if it gets past your towers, you are screwed.

I finished the first part of the game. The best way to progress is to get all the stars from each level and use them. This means getting all 5 stars per level. This makes later levels much easier to complete. I did this by doing the first 10 levels and getting at least 2 stars. Then I went back and did all the levels to get 5 stars. Use a basic strategy of using a mix of archers, wizards, and cannons where mobs come in. Troops near the kingdom. Game became easy.

Enforced to use items to beat the special challenges of each map. So either spend hours to grind enough gems to buy a bunch of items to beat one challenge, or drop the buck. It is annoying, but otherwise decent game. I like the later ones more personally. The fact that you can't at least use your hero for those special challenges in this one, makes it too hard in my opinion.

star_5.text: FINALLY! A good fulfilling game on play pass! IMO the selection has been lacking. This review is for the free no ads version on play pass. It is a really fun strategy game! It has good tutorials & an optional strategy guide to help pass each level. Each flag on the map has 3 games w/ 3 difficulty levels. That's basically 9 games in each flag! Idk how many flags yet, but it looks like a big map. Really great strategy game. It's a great brain teaser. Wonderful game! Great work devs!

Just finished on veteran and got all the achievements and iron/heroic stages done. It's a really really good game and I wasn't sure about the lack of towers unlike other games I played but turns out it's really fun to do many different strategies. They all have their niches and flaws and pros. The same goes for heroes. I feel they are very well balanced where even the best ones have their flaws. I personally prefer the leveling system of frontiers and on but still really had fun with this game.

Played this like 12 years ago. Still good now. Ideal sort of free game: almost as polished as a pay game - not a watered down experience. They used this game to sell several sequels, and that's where the profit came from. So if you finish this game and want more, they do have more you can buy. I wish more developers could follow this model, instead of monetizing by either cramming in tons of ads, making games pay to win, or producing a very low quality game and still cramming in constant ads.

Exactly what is advertised. I'm telling you, I have scoured the play store looking for legitimately good games... this is of the VERY few good games... great art style, very juicy, crunchy gameplay, engaging gameplay, unlockables, achievements, easy to understand... I could go on and on. Bravo, I commend the design and development team for an incredible job on this game.

This is the first legitimate tower defense game I've found on the play store. It plays just like the old flash games on the computer in my youth, and gives zero pressure to purchase anything. No ads, no hassles, just pure nostalgic enjoyment, which is refreshing in this day and age. Love it! Especially love all the little Easter eggs and hidden achievements you can find, it's so fun!

I didn't think I was going to enjoy this game as much as I do. It's more strategic most TD games, with a bit more freedom. Example, I played a level three times using different tower, placed in different areas, had different results and won 2 out 3. So far earning currencies seems reasonable and to receive more you have a choice of watching ads. Oh, NO FORCED ADS... SO FAR.

As someone who has played these games on PC for years (RIP flash), it translates well as a mobile game. There are a few hours worth of gameplay, maybe more if you are a completionist. There are hidden bits of comedy to be found by clicking on the stage environments, which doesn't hurt. Overall, I am satisfied with my purchase.

Great game. I've been playing for around 3 months and haven't experienced any kind of glitches, loss of progress, or lagging. Don't really have any ad interruptions or ads between levels. It's challenging and moves along quickly. Easy to understand, not too many things going on that cause distractions. The only issue some people might have is how challenging it can get. It feels like you have to buy items to defeat some levels. It's possible to beat them without purchases, but it gets hard.

It's a very well-made game, with minimal ads, interesting and varied gameplay. It's definitely a big step above most mobile tower defense games. It's just really hard! Even on easy, I'm struggling to get past "the dark tower." I even followed the walkthrough (1st time this game) but enemies kept breaking past. I started to get really frustrated but eventually with the right upgrades I was able to continue. Definitely a good game for those who like a challenge.

This game is awesome. No ads, little to no spending and great game play. Like others said, the levels do get quite difficult with time, but it makes for a challenging opportunity. The only dislike I have is that the hero gets selected by default(or by accident) when in a battle. I try to click on a tower and I get the X saying that I can't move my hero there, but All I wanted to do was to select the tower and not the hero.

I don't usually rate games, but I have to say this is one of the best tower defense/strategy games out there. Ironhide never disappoints and always brings quality entertainment. Many have tried to copy their style - none have succeeded. The monetization isn't in your face and you can fully enjoy the game(s) without spending a single extra dime. I would recommend purchasing their entire catalog of games. The art is quirky and sharp, the color is rich and vibrant, and the animation is topnotch. :D

This game is great! I played through on easy level, there were no ads and it wasn't necessary to purchase anything. It's fairly simple gameplay and intuitive as well but there are enough options to offer diverse play styles. I made a purchase at the end just to show support since it's seems so rare to find a game that isn't pay to win. This one is worth a try if you like tower defense. One issue: if I paused a game and the phone went off, the game couldn't be revived.

Funnest tower defense game I've played. The gameplay, characters, and right engagements, down to the 1x1 unit levels are spot on. Really well done. Furthermore, there is a ton of variety in options, maps, and new units. A lot of work went into this. I'd really like to see a x2, x3 speed option. Some of the battles take a little bit of a time commitment if the units are slow moving.

Hands down! this is the quintessential game when it comes to mobile games. A classic tower defense game , made special by absolutely fantastic art style. Not to mention its solid game play, responsive controls and an overall charm as you take down wave, after wave of enemies. You get the sense the studio not only wanted to make this game, but also had a fun while they were in production. This is THE tower defense game for mobile.

Game was definitely not meant to be a cash grab. Love that. Every single level (including all Iron and Heroic) is beatable without an enemy or extra. Took a long time to do so but just great timing and tower mix. Overall it's a very fun game, great music. Not liking how to the screen jumps to another part of the screen sometimes when I click to put either rain of fire or soldiers somewhere. And it zooms in at the bottom of the screen when building. Completely unnecessary. Still 5 stars though.

A very fun game that has quite a bit of interactive battle elements in addition to the typical add/upgrade elements. It helps a lot to pay attention to what enemies arrive in a level and plan accordingly (such as needing armor weakening for heavily armored enemies). It also helps to not rush through all the lower levels, as things can get pretty difficult when you've progressed through to some of the boss-inclusive levels. I got a little frustrated at one point, but worked my way through it.

I love this game! one of the best tower defense games ever put to design by far. the style and art direction is instantly recognizable while keeping things readable. the only negative I have is the heroes. the very pay to win nature of the heroes is slightly disappointing. I just hope that doesn't become a bigger thing in later games. thankfully the prices are pretty fair.

I've played this game for a long time and it has never failed me. With no ads, it makes this game free, fast, and fun. I have seen and would love to see more new stuff, but what is has is stll great. I do wish, after playing it recently, that it was easier to beat the Shaman and Ogres from the beggining stage. I also wish that the fireball starts off with more basic damage. Other than that, this game never dissapoints. I think you'll like it.

Compared to other tower defense games; kingdom rush has consistently been at the top of the genre, right alongside "bloons tower defense". This game has all the right things to make it stay at the top, even here on the first game in the series: nostalgia, replayability, good graphics, quirky art style, great gameplay, and a lot of range as far as enemy types, locales, and tower types go. This game is absolutely one of my favorite td titles of all time, I'd recommend it to any fan of the genre

It's FREE, and offers what feels like a very fine tuned tower defense experience that offers variety and challenge, coupled with the fun addition of the player controlled heroes to help cover weak points and mix things up in the gameplay. Once I've started, it was hard to stop until the end. The payable extra hero classes are surely fun and powerful, but you absolutely do not require them to beat any level, difficulty, or challenge. You just need wits and determination (and a guide sometimes..)
